To determine the strongest leather among the options provided, we must evaluate the physical properties of each material. Pig skin is known for its durability and toughness due to its dense fiber structure, making it stronger than sheep skin (which is soft/delicate) or imitation leather (which is synthetic). While "Roan" typically refers to a type of coloring or a specific finish, pig skin is the most recognized durable natural leather in this context.

\boxed{C}
